q: Why is distributed monitoring important in modern systems?
opts: To prevent cascading failures and detect issues early

q: What is a cascading failure in distributed systems?
opts: A single failure causing dependent services to fail

Why are alert managers used in monitoring systems?
opts: To notify administrators when rules are violated

Why is service discovery important in distributed monitoring?
opts: Services scale dynamically and endpoints change

q: What problem can occur with a single monitoring server?
opts: It creates a single point of failure

q: What is a major advantage of the pull-based monitoring model?
opts: It prevents network congestion and decouples applications
It removes the need for storage

q: What is a disadvantage of storing monitoring data forever?
opts: It removes the need for databases

opts: To improve scalability across data centers

q: What happens in the local pull stage of hybrid monitoring?
opts: Secondary monitoring servers scrape local nodes

q: What is the purpose of the global monitoring layer?
opts: To aggregate monitoring data from multiple data centers

opts: To identify communication delays between systems
